questions.q: The wettability of a surface by a liquid depends primarily on
options: angle of contact between the surface and the liquid
questions.solution: The value of angle of contact determines whether a liquid will spread on the surface.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The velocity of a small ball of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> and density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ρ</mi></math>, when dropped in a container filled with glycerine becomes constant after some time. If the density of glycerine is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>ρ</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>, then the viscous force acting on the ball will be:
questions.solution:
When velocity becomes constant then,

Fv+Fb=Mg

Fv=Mg-Fb

=Vρbg-Vρgg

=Vgρ-ρ2

=Vgρ2

=(Vρ)g2=Mg2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Scent sprayer is based on
questions.solution: A scent sprayer is an example of fall in pressure due to increase in velocity. Also as per Bernoulli's theorem that datum head, pressure head and velocity head of a flowing liquid is constant. Therefore scent sprayer is based on Bernoulli's theorem.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A small hole of area cross-section <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> is present near the bottom of a fully filled open tank of height <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Taking <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, the rate of flow of water through the open hole would be nearly
questions.solution:
Velocity of efflux v=2gh
v=2×10×2
=2×10
Rate of flow = area ×v
=2×10-6×2×10
=4×3.14×10-6
≅12.6×10-6
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The property utilized in the manufacture of lead shots is
questions.solution: The property utilized in the manufacture of lead shots is surface tension of liquid lead. In this process, molten lead is made to pass through a sieve from a high tower and allowed to fall in water. The molten lead particles, while descending, assume a spherical shape and solidify in this form, before falling into water.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Viscous force exerted by the liquid flowing between two plates in a streamline flow depends upon the
options: velocity gradient in the direction perpendicular to the plates
co-efficient of viscosity of the liquid
questions.solution:
Viscous force exerted by the liquid flowing between two plates is given by
$$
F=-\eta A \frac{d v}{d y}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A cylinder of height $1 \mathrm{~m}$ is filled with water. If a small orifice is made at a height of $0.25 \mathrm{~m}$ from the ground, the horizontal range of water stream will be
questions.solution:
$v=\sqrt{2 g h}=\sqrt{2 \times 10 \times 0.75}=\sqrt{15} \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$
Horizontal range,
$\begin{aligned} & R=\sqrt{\frac{2 h}{g}} \times \sqrt{15}=\sqrt{\frac{2 \times 0.25 \times 15}{10}}=\sqrt{\frac{15}{20}}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{4}} \\ & =\frac{1.732}{2}=0.866 \mathrm{~m}=86.6 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two non-mixing liquids of densities <math><mi mathvariant="normal">ρ</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">ρ</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> are put in a container. The height of each liquid is <math><mn>h.</mn></math> A solid cylinder of length <math> <mtext>L</mtext> </math> and density <math><mn>d</mn></math> is put in this container. The cylinder floats with its axis vertical and length <math><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> in the denser liquid. The density <math><mn>d</mn></math> is equal to:
questions.solution:
f0=mg
ρA1-pLg+nρApLg=dALg
ρ1-p+nρp=d
1-p+npρ=d
1+n-1pρ=d
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A small sphere of radius <math> <mi>r</mi> </math> falls from rest in a viscous liquid. As a result, heat is produced due to viscous force. The rate of production of heat when the sphere attains terminal velocity, is proportional to
questions.solution:
Rate of heat produced = power of viscous force
terminal velocity V T = 2 r 2 ( ρ−σ )g 9η
⇒ V T ∝ r 2
dQ dt =6πηr  V T × V T
⇒ dQ dt ∝ r 5
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A U tube with both ends open to the atmosphere is partially filled with water. Oil, which is immiscible with water, is poured into one side until it stands at a distance of 10 mm above the water level on the other side. Meanwhile, the water rises by 65 mm from its original level (see diagram). The density of the oil is____ρw=1000 kg m-3
questions.solution:
∵ Pressure in a stationary liquid at the same level is the same everywhere. As both ends of the tube are open, the pressure on both free surfaces should be equal.
hoil ρoil g=hwater  ρwaterg 
140×ρoil=130×ρwater
ρoil=928 kg m-3.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A cylindrical tank is filled with water to level of $3 \mathrm{~m}$. A hole is opened at height of $52.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ from bottom. The ratio of the area of the hole to that of cross-sectional area of the cylinder is 0.1 . The square of the speed with which water is coming out from the orifice is (Take $g=10 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-2}$ )
questions.solution:
Suppose $A$ be the area of cross section of tank, $a$ be the area of hole, $v_e$ be the velocity of efflux, $h$ be the height of liquid above the hole,
Let $v$ be the speed with which the level decreases in the container. Using equation of continuity, we get $a v_e=A v \quad$ or $\quad v=\frac{a v_e}{A}$ Using Bernoulli's theorem, we have $\begin{aligned} & P_0+h \rho g+\frac{1}{2} \rho v^2=P_0+\frac{1}{2} \rho v_e^2 \\ & h \rho g+\frac{1}{2} \rho\left(\frac{a v_e}{A}\right)^2=\frac{1}{2} \rho v_e^2 \\ & \text { or } \quad v_e^2=\frac{2 h g}{1-\left(a^2 / A^2\right)}=\frac{2 \times(3-0.525) \times 10}{1-(0.1)^2} \\ & \quad=50 \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~s}^{-2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.solution:
Mg=2Tl

π r 2 lρg=2Tl

r 2 = 2T πρg
r= 2T πρg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A vessel completely filled with water has holes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> at depths <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>h</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>h</mi></math> from the top respectively. The hole <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is a square of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> is a circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math>. The water flowing out per second from both the holes is same. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
A 1 V 1 = A 1 V 2
L 2 2 gh = π r 2 6 gh
L 4 gh = π 2 r 4 3 gh
L = π 1 2 r 3 1 4
L = r π 1 2 3 1 4
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A certain number of spherical liquid drops of radius $r$ coalesce to form a single drop of radius $R$ and volume $V$. If $T$ is the surface tension of the liquid, which one of the following statements is true for the energy $(E)$ in the process?
questions.solution:
Change in surface energy is given by
$E=T(\Delta A) \quad---(1)$
The initial area is given by,
$\mathrm{A}=\left(4 \pi r^2\right) n$
The final area is given by,
$\mathrm{a}=4 \pi R^2$
Therefore, the change in area is given by,
$\begin{aligned}
& \Delta A=a-A \\
& \Rightarrow \Delta A=4 \pi\left(n r^2-R^2\right)---(2)
\end{aligned}$
Now, using volume conservation: $\left(\frac{4}{3} \pi r^2\right) n=\frac{4}{3} R^3$
$\therefore n=\frac{R^3}{r^3} \quad---(3)$
$\Delta A=4 \pi\left[\frac{R^3}{r^3} \cdot r^2-R^2\right]=4 \pi\left[\frac{R^3}{r}-\frac{R^3}{R}\right]=\left(\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3\right) 3\left[\frac{1}{r}-\frac{1}{R}\right]=3 V\left[\frac{1}{r}-\frac{1}{R}\right]$
Introducing above value in equation (1)
$E=3 V T\left[\frac{1}{r}-\frac{1}{R}\right]$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> of the soap bubble is doubled under isothermal condition. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math> be the surface tension of the soap bubble, the work done in doing so is given by
questions.solution:
The surface energy of a bubble
E = Tension × surface area
E=T×A
For soap bubble
E=2T×A
E=2T×4πR2
Here, R = radius of the bubble
If the radius is doubled then we have
E′=2T×4πR2
E′=2T×4π2R2
E′=32πR2T

Work done = E'-E

⇒ 32πR2T-8πR2T=24πR2T
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A spherical solid ball of volume $V$ is made up of material of density $\rho$. It is falling through a liquid of density $\sigma(\sigma<\rho)$. Assume that the liquid applies a viscous force on the ball that is proportional to square of the terminal speed $v_{\mathrm{T}}, F=-K v_{\mathrm{T}}^2, \forall(K>0)$, then the terminal speed of the ball is ( $g=$ acceleration due to gravity)
questions.solution:
The condition for terminal speed $v_{\mathrm{T}}$ is given by,
weight $(W)=$ buoyant force $(f)+$ viscous force $(F)$
We know, $W=\rho V g, f=\sigma V g$ and $F=K v_{\mathrm{T}}^2$
$\therefore \rho V g=\sigma V g+K v_{\mathrm{T}}^2$
$v_{\mathrm{T}}=\sqrt{\frac{(\rho-\sigma) V g}{K}}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: By sucking through a straw, a student can reduce the pressure in his lungs to $750 \mathrm{~mm}$ of $\mathrm{Hg}$ (density $=13.6 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^3$ ). Using the straw, he can drink water from a glass upto a maximum depth of
questions.solution:
Pressure difference between lungs and atmosphere $=760 \mathrm{~mm}-750 \mathrm{~mm}=10 \mathrm{~mm}=1 \mathrm{~cm}$ of $\mathrm{Hg}$.
Pressure difference $=1 \times 13.6 \times g=l \times 1 \times g$ of water
i.e., one can draw from a depth of $13.6 \mathrm{~cm}$ of water.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A solid sphere of volume $V$ and density $\rho$ floats at the interface of two immiscible liquids of densities $\rho_1$ and $\rho_2$ respectively. If $\rho_1 < \rho < \rho_2$, then the ratio of volume of the parts of the sphere in upper and lower liquid is
questions.solution:
$V=$ Volume of solid sphere. Let $V_1=$ Volume of the part of the sphere immersed in a liquid of density $\rho_1$ and $V_2=$ Volume of the part of the sphere immersed in liquid of density $\rho_2$. According to law of floatation,
Hence from eqns (i) and (ii), $\begin{aligned} & V_1 \rho g+V_2 \rho g=V_1 \rho_1 g+V_2 \rho_2 g \\ & \text { or } V_1\left(\rho-\rho_1\right) g=V_2\left(\rho_2-\rho\right) g \\ & \text { or } \frac{V_1}{V_2}=\frac{\rho_2-\rho}{\rho-\rho_1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A closed tube filled with water is rotating uniformly in a horizontal plane about the axis OO' as shown in the figure. The manometers A and B which are fixed on the tube at distances r1 and r2, indicate pressures P1 and P2 respectively. The angular velocity (ω) of the tube is
questions.solution:
Consider the condition of equilibrium for the mass of water contained between cross section separated by x and x+dx from rotation axis.

dp=ρω2xdx ;

∫p1p2dp=∫r1r2ρω2xdx




p2-p1=ρω2x22r1r2=ρω2r22-r122

ω=2P2-P1ρr22-r12
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The heart of a man pumps <math> <mn>5</mn> </math> litres of blood through the arteries per minute at a pressure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>150</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> of mercury. If the density of mercury be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13.6</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>3</mn></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, then the power of heart in watt is:
questions.solution: \(\begin{aligned} & \text { Pressure }=\mathrm{P}=150 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg} \\ & \text { Pumping rate }=\frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{5 \times 10^{-3}}{60} \mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{s} \\ & \text { Power of heart }=\text { P. } \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\rho \mathrm{gh} \times \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dt}} \\ & =\left(13.6 \times 10^3\right)(10) \times(0.15) \times \frac{5 \times 10^{-3}}{60} \\ & =\frac{13.6 \times 5 \times 0.15}{6}=1.70 \mathrm{watt}\end{aligned}\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
